domain:
In an academic institution, students enroll in various courses offered by the university. Each student is uniquely identified by a student code. An enrollment in the course requires the specific date on which it occurred for administrative purposes.

Courses are supported by Teaching Assistants (TAs), who are identified by their first and last names. A TA may assist in multiple different courses, and the specific role of a TA within a course is defined by an assignment, which describes the details and responsibilities assigned to them. Regarding courses logistics, they consist of one or more lab sections. Each section occurs at a specific location and time slot with start and end times.

questions.question:
Is university a relevant domain concept that should be included in the model?

For example:
1. University is a relevant concept in this domain and should be represented in the model.
2. University does not correspond to any relevant domain concept and should be removed from the model.

Which option is better for you?
questions.option_1: Option 1: Keep university, it is a relevant domain concept.
questions.option_2: Option 2: Remove university, it is not relevant.
questions.selected: Option 1: Keep university, it is a relevant domain concept.

questions.question:
Should university be modeled as a separate class associated with Course, or as a simple representation within Course?

For example:
1. University is a separate class with attributes: . This allows users to query and filter by individual university properties.
2. University is modeled as a simple represntation in Course. This is suitable when university details are not needed.
Which option is better for you?
questions.option_1: Option 1: The University is a separate class with its own attributes.
questions.option_2: Option 2: The university is a simple representation in Course.
questions.selected: Option 2: The university is a simple representation in Course.

questions.question:
Should the university in course be modeled as a an attribute which allows free text input, or as an enumeration with predefined literals?

For example:
1. Use university as string attribute in course, which allows any text value.
2. Use university as an enumeration which uses predefined values in literals: university or academic institution.

Which option is better for you?
questions.option_1: Option 1: String attribute that allows any text value for University.
questions.option_2: Option 2: Enumeration University with predefined values: literal 1, literal 2 and literal 3.
questions.selected: Option 1: String attribute that allows any text value for University.

questions.option_1: Option 1: Keep role, it is relevant information for assignment.
questions.option_2: Option 2: Remove role, it is not a relevant information.

questions.question:

For example:
1. Details is a separate class with its own attributes. This allows users to query and filter by individual details properties.
2. Details is modeled as a simple representation in Assignment. This is suitable when details details are not needed.

Which option is better for you?
questions.option_1: Option 1: The Details is a separate class with its own attributes.
questions.option_2: Option 2: The details is a simple representation in Assignment.
questions.selected: Option 2: The details is a simple representation in Assignment.

questions.question:
Should the details in assignment be modeled as a an attribute which allows free text input, or as an enumeration with predefined literals?

For example:
1. Use details as string attribute in assignment, which allows any text value.
2. Use details as an enumeration which uses predefined values in literals: grading, lab supervision, recitation lead, office hours, tutoring, exam proctoring, content preparation or administrative support.

Which option is better for you?
questions.option_1: Option 1: String attribute that allows any text value for details.
questions.option_2: Option 2: Enumeration details with predefined values: literal 1, literal 2 and literal 3.
questions.selected: Option 1: String attribute that allows any text value for details.

questions.option_1: Option 1: The Responsibilities is a separate class with its own attributes.
questions.option_2: Option 2: The responsibilities is a simple representation in Assignment.

questions.option_2: Option 2: Enumeration responsibilities with predefined values: literal 1, literal 2 and literal 3.

2. Use time slot as an enumeration which uses predefined values in literals: morning, afternoon or evening.

Which option is better for you?
questions.option_1: Option 1: String attribute that allows any text value for TimeSlot.
questions.option_2: Option 2: Enumeration TimeSlot with predefined values: literal 1, literal 2 and literal 3.
questions.selected: Option 1: String attribute that allows any text value for TimeSlot.

questions.question:
Should Enrollment be modeled as an association class linking Student and Course, or as a regular class with two separate associations?

For example:
1. Association class: Enrollment exists only as part of the relationship between Student and Course. A enrollment cannot exist without the link between Student and Course.
2. Regular class: Enrollment is a class that has links to Student and Course. In this case multiple enrollments can exist for every Student and Course.


Which option is better for you?
questions.option_1: Option 1: Association class, Enrollment as association class linking Student and Course.
questions.option_2: Option 2: Regular class, Enrollment as separate class with two associations.
questions.selected: Option 1: Association class, Enrollment as association class linking Student and Course.

questions.question:
Should Assignment be modeled as an association class linking TeachingAssistant and Course, or as a regular class with two separate associations?

For example:
1. Association class: Assignment exists only as part of the relationship between TeachingAssistant and Course. A assignment cannot exist without the link between TeachingAssistant and Course.
2. Regular class: Assignment is a class that has links to TeachingAssistant and Course. In this case multiple assignments can exist for every TeachingAssistant and Course.


Which option is better for you?
questions.option_1: Option 1: Association class, Assignment as association class linking TeachingAssistant and Course.
questions.option_2: Option 2: Regular class, Assignment as separate class with two associations.
questions.selected: Option 1: Association class, Assignment as association class linking TeachingAssistant and Course.
